title: Public signal field overflow allows Solidity/circuit semantic mismatch

artifacts.description: Add BN254 scalar field bound constant (r = 21888242871839275222246405745257275088548364400416034343698204186575808495617) and require(pubSignal < FIELD_MODULUS) checks for all public inputs (sessionNonce, currentTimestamp, humanMerkleRoot, nullifierHash, nonceBinding, agentCommitment, permissionBits, expiry) before passing to verifier contracts.

Minimal library exporting FIELD_MODULUS constant and assertInField(uint256) helper. Centralizes the bound so HumanUniquenessVerifier, AgentPolicyVerifier, and DelegationVerifier wrappers can all import from one source.

Hardhat tests: (1) submit sessionNonce = n (valid, succeeds), (2) submit sessionNonce = n + r (reverts with FieldOverflow), (3) submit currentTimestamp = 2^256-1 (reverts), (4) submit each public signal at FIELD_MODULUS-1 (boundary pass), confirming nonce deduplication is not bypassable via modular wrap.

Certora Prover CVL rule: for any call to verifyProof(), assert that all public signal arguments are strictly less than FIELD_MODULUS. Also assert that two distinct uint256 values that differ by r cannot both reach the verifier (proving usedNonces cannot be bypassed).
Certora configuration file: links IdentityRegistry + FieldBoundLib with the spec, sets solc version, loop unrolling, and optimistic fallback settings.
Short security note documenting the BN254 field bound invariant, which contract functions enforce it, Certora rule reference, and why the check must happen before the verifier call (not inside it).

steps: Identify every public input accepted by IdentityRegistry: sessionNonce, currentTimestamp, humanMerkleRoot, nullifierHash, nonceBinding, agentCommitment, permissionBits, expiry — confirm list against circuit public output declarations in HumanUniqueness.circom, AgentPolicy.circom, Delegation.circom.

Create contracts/src/FieldBoundLib.sol exporting FIELD_MODULUS = 21888242871839275222246405745257275088548364400416034343698204186575808495617 and an assertInField(uint256 v) function that reverts with a named error FieldModulusExceeded(uint256 value) if v >= FIELD_MODULUS.

Import FieldBoundLib in IdentityRegistry.sol; call assertInField() on every public input parameter at the top of each external function that accepts public signals (proveHandshake, verifyHandshake, registerHuman, registerAgent, etc.) before any other logic including usedNonces checks.

Write Hardhat tests in contracts/test/FieldOverflow.test.ts covering: valid n passes, n+r reverts, 2^256-1 reverts, each individual signal at boundary (FIELD_MODULUS-1) passes, confirm usedNonces[n] is set and usedNonces[n+r] cannot bypass it.
Run npm run test:contracts to confirm all new tests pass and no existing tests regress.

Write Certora spec contracts/certora/specs/FieldBound.spec with two rules: (1) publicSignalsInField — for all reachable states, any public signal arg reaching verifier.verifyProof() is < FIELD_MODULUS; (2) nonceNonBypassable — there is no pair (n, n+r) both accepted as distinct nonces in usedNonces.
Create contracts/certora/confs/FieldBound.conf and run certoraRun to verify both rules hold; iterate on spec if counterexamples surface.
Add a note to SECURITY.md under 'Field Arithmetic Invariants' cross-referencing FieldBoundLib and the Certora spec.
Write docs/security/field-overflow-mitigation.md explaining the BN254 scalar field gap, the attack vector (nonce wrap), and the fix.
Commit with DCO sign-off: git commit -s -m 'fix(contracts): reject public signals >= BN254 field modulus to prevent nonce bypass'
dependencies: Certora Prover CLI (certoraRun) with valid API key
Existing verifier contracts (HumanUniquenessVerifier, AgentPolicyVerifier, DelegationVerifier) generated from current .zkey files
Public input lists from HumanUniqueness.circom, AgentPolicy.circom, Delegation.circom
